hypothesis: A description that leads with the time-saving benefit (not money) combined with a 'try it free' CTA will outperform current copy by appealing to busy shoppers who value convenience over savings.

changes.hero_description: Skip the coupon hunting. CouponSnap checks 1,000+ stores automatically at checkout — in under 3 seconds. Same savings, zero effort. Unlike Honey, we never touch your data.

rationale.hero_description: 'Skip the coupon hunting' opens with eliminating effort — appeals to convenience-motivated users, not just deal-hunters. '3 seconds' adds specificity to the speed claim. 'Same savings, zero effort' is a memorable two-part value prop. Ends with privacy claim to maintain differentiator.
rationale.cta_primary: 'Try' is lower-commitment than 'Add' or 'Get'. 'CouponSnap Free' = brand + pricing in 2 words. Tests whether softer trial language ('try') vs commitment language ('get') affects click rate.
